'''Adari''' was a [[Mountain/Legends|mountainous]], [[Volcano/Legends|volcanic]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] located in the [[Inner Rim/Legends|Inner Rim's]] [[Adari sector/Legends|Adari sector]]. It was the [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of the [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Adarian/Legends|Adarians]], although [[Archaeologist/Legends|archaeologists]] believed evidence pointed to the [[Species/Legends|species]] not being native to the planet. The earliest Adarians on Adari spread outward from a single [[Unidentified Adari region|region]] of the planet, already having a single shared culture by the [[Time/Legends|time]].

Such cultural homogeneity among the Adarians allowed the species to rapidly develop [[Ore/Legends|ore]] [[Mining/Legends|mining]] and smelting and [[Nuclear fission|atomic-fission]] industries and technologies. A planet-wide [[Adarian government|corporate government]] was formed, and the species came to be divided by a rigid [[Sceel'sara|caste system]].

Eventually, the Adarians expanded outward into the [[Adari system/Legends|Adari system]], and the discovery of [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]] technology led to the species controlling the entire Adari [[Sector/Legends|sector]] and joining the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] [[Millennium/Legends|millennium]] before the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]]. Adari later signed a non-aggression treaty with the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] and supplied it with raw materials necessary for [[Starship/Legends|starship]] construction, and the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] eventually established a presence on the planet.

==Description==
Adari was a [[Planet/Legends|planet]]<ref name="GG12" /> located in the [[Adari system/Legends|Adari system]], a part of the [[Adari sector/Legends|Adari sector]]<ref name="EAOC" /> in [[The Slice/Legends|the Slice]] portion of<ref name="TEA" /> the [[Inner Rim/Legends|Inner Rim]].<ref name="EAOC" /> The [[local year]] on Adari lasted 409 [[Standard day/Legends|standard days]]. Adari was a [[Volcano/Legends|volcanic]] world, the surface of which mostly featured towering [[Mountain/Legends|mountains]] that were heavily snow-covered for almost two-thirds of the local [[Standard year/Legends|year]].<ref name="GG12" />

==History==
===The origin of Adarians===
Since the oldest [[City/Legends|settlements]] of the [[Adarian/Legends|Adarian]] [[Species/Legends|species]] were concentrated in a single [[Unidentified Adari region|region]] of Adari, and since there was no fossil record that would indicate the existence of an evolutionary ancestor species, [[Archaeologist/Legends|archaeologists]] considered it likely that the Adarians arrived on Adari from another [[Star system/Legends|star system]]<ref name="GG12" /> [[Millennium/Legends|millennia]] before the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]].<ref name="UAA" />

From the region containing their original settlements, the Adarians spread out across their planet while already having a similar shared culture. Due to not spending labor and materials on internal warfare, they rapidly developed technologies that allowed to exploit their [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld's]] wealth of [[Mineral/Legends|minerals]] and [[Ore/Legends|ores]].<ref name="GG12" />

===Outward expansion===
Early on, the Adarians developed [[Mining/Legends|mining]], smelting, construction, and [[Nuclear fission|atomic-fission]] industries that allowed them to expand out into their [[Star/Legends|star]] system and harvest most of its [[Asteroid/Legends|asteroids]]. When the Adarians discovered [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]] technology, they used their experience as [[Realspace/Legends|deep-space]] asteroid [[Miner/Legends|miners]], ore smelters, and [[Freighter/Legends|freighter]] builders to expand their commercial interests in other systems throughout the<ref name="GG12" /> Adari [[Sector/Legends|sector]],<ref name="EAOC" /> steadily increasing the wealth of Adari itself.<ref name="GG12" />

Eventually, the Adarians were contacted by the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. The species quickly became a member of that government,<ref name="GG12" /> with [[Unidentified Adarian Republic officer|one Adarian]] serving in the [[Republic Military/Legends|Republic Military]]<ref name="FatalAlliance">''[[The Old Republic: Fatal Alliance]]''</ref> around [[3643 BBY]].<ref name="3643 BBY">''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' dates the events of ''[[The Old Republic: Fatal Alliance]]'', which includes the [[Unidentified Adarian Republic officer|Adarian's]] appearance, to approximately [[3643 BBY]].</ref>

===Adari and the Empire===
Adari maintained its neutrality and formal independence from the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], which coveted the planet's natural and technological resources, by being one of the few worlds to sign a non-aggression treaty with the Empire. Under the treaty, however, the planet still provided the Empire with vast amounts of raw materials necessary for [[Starship/Legends|starship]] construction. In addition, it was believed that the Empire would likely exert [[Politics/Legends|political]] pressure on Adari to deal with a movement that had formed in the lower castes of the Adarian society. The Adarian agitators called for the caste system of their species' hierarchical society to shift from being [[Reproduction/Legends|birth]]-determined to being meritocratic.<ref name="GG12" />

{{RPGseed}}
During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], agents of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] established a foothold on Adari by exploiting the discontent of the lower Adarian castes with the social system of their homeworld.<ref name="GG12" />
{{Endgame}}

==Inhabitants==
{{Main|Adarian/Legends}}
[[File:Adarian_UAA.jpg|thumb|right|180px|Adari served as the homeworld of Adarians.]]
Adari was the homeworld of the Adarians, a species of [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoids]] whose most distinguished physical characteristic was a hole perforating their [[Bone/Legends|skull]] from one side to the other. The Adarians were adapted to a [[Diet/Legends|diet]] consisting of [[Nut/Legends|nuts]] and thick-skinned [[Fruit/Legends|fruit]],<ref name="GG12" /> and they spoke the [[Adarese]] [[Language/Legends|language]].<ref name="UAA" />

Adarian society was divided by a rigid caste system known as [[sceel'sara]]. Their homeworld, Adari, was ruled by a single planet-wide [[Adarian government|government]] that functioned as a large corporation, controlling and managing the resources and labor force of the entire planet for the profit of the Adarian species as a whole. In addition, the Adarian government owned and controlled all Adarian companies.<ref name="GG12" />

Despite the Adarians controlling an entire space sector and having business holdings and industrial operations in hundreds of systems both in that sector and elsewhere, the species remained focused on their homeworld. Most Adarians tended to maintain at least a temporary residence on Adari, and off-world members of the species tried to visit the planet at least twice per local year.<ref name="GG12" />

==Locations==
The [[Hall of Decisions]] was a multi-floored arcology on Adari. The headquarters of the Adarian government were located in the structure, and the majority of the Adarian species was [[Administrator/Legends|administered]] from there.<ref name="GG12" />

One small geographical area of Adari predominantly featured broken hills and [[Water/Legends|freshwater]] lakes, which made it one of the most habitable areas on the planet in comparison with the high, snow-covered mountains covering most of the world. According to archaeological records, the region at one point contained the oldest settlements of the Adarian species.<ref name="GG12" />

==Behind the scenes==
Adari was created by [[Lisa Smedman]] and introduced in ''[[Galaxy Guide 12: Aliens — Enemies and Allies]]'', a [[1995]] [[West End Games]] sourcebook. The book includes a [[roleplaying game]] adventure seed for use with ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''. This article assumes the scenario plays out as described.<ref name="GG12" /> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Adari system, and therefore the planet Adari, in grid square M-9.<ref name="TEA" />
